sentences.grammarNote: 'Acabar de + infinitive' = to have just done something. 'Acabo de recibir' = I have just received. 'Acaba de llegar' = He/she has just arrived. It always refers to something that happened moments ago.

sentences.base_language: They are very proud of me.
My mother cries tears of joy.
We celebrate with a dinner at a restaurant.
sentences.target_language: Celebramos con una cena en un restaurante.

sentences.base_language: I feel grateful and hopeful about the future.
sentences.target_language: Me siento agradecido y esperanzado por el futuro.
sentences.grammarNote: 'Por' expresses reason or cause here: 'esperanzado por el futuro' = hopeful about/because of the future. Compare 'para', which expresses purpose or destination: 'trabajo para el futuro' (I work for the future / toward the future).
